revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.comment: Auto-generated page using script written by Immanuelle, either as a placeholder redirect or to make bots recognize the page as made by Immanuelle
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Kamo_Tsuba_Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{神社| 名称=鴨都波神社| 所在地=奈良県御所市宮前町514| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|27|35|N|135|43|57|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=積羽八重事代主命
下照姫命| 社格=式内社（名神大）・県社| 創建=崇神天皇の時代| 本殿=加茂[[流造]]| 例祭=[[10月9日]]}} '''鴨都波神社'''（かもつわじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[御所市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==祭神== 積羽八重事代主命（[[事代主]]）と[[シタテルヒメ|下照姫命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[建御名方命]]を配祀する。[[葛城氏]]・[[鴨氏]]によって祀られた神社で、[[高鴨神社]]（高鴨社）・[[葛城御歳神社]]（中鴨社）に対して「下鴨社」とも呼ばれる。事代主神は元々は鴨族が信仰していた神であり、当社が事代主神の信仰の本源である。[[大神神社]]（奈良県[[桜井市]]）に祀られる[[大国主]]の子に当たることから、「大神神社の別宮」とも称される。 下照姫命については、事代主とともに祀られることに疑問があることから、元は別の神が祀られていたものとみられる。当社の古い社名は「鴨弥都波（かもみつは）」であり、「鴨の水際（みづは）の神」と解せる。当地は葛城川と柳田川の合流地点であり、元々は水の神を祀っていたものとする説がある。また、事代主の妹である[[高照姫命]]が祀られていたのが下照姫命と混同されたとする説もある。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[崇神天皇]]の時代、勅命により[[太田田根子]]の孫の大賀茂都美命が創建した。一帯は「鴨都波遺跡」という遺跡で、弥生時代の土器や農具が多数出土しており、古くから鴨族がこの地に住みついて農耕をしていたことがわかる。 [[延喜式神名帳]]では「鴨都波八重事代主命神社 二座」と記載され、名神大社に列している。[[延喜式]]巻三「臨時祭」では「鴨神社 二座」と記されている。 大正10年（[[1927年]]）に県社に列格した。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~kamotuba/ 鴨都波神社]（公式サイト） [[Category:奈良県の神社|かもつわしんしや]] [[Category:名神大社|かもつわしんしや]] [[Category:府県社|かもつわしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=鴨都波神社| 画像=[[File:Kamotsuba-jinja haiden.jpg|220px]]| 所在地=奈良県御所市宮前町514| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|27|35|N|135|43|57|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=積羽八重事代主命
下照姫命| 社格=式内社（名神大）・県社| 創建=崇神天皇の時代| 本殿=加茂[[流造]]| 例祭=[[10月9日]]}} '''鴨都波神社'''（かもつばじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[御所市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == 積羽八重事代主命（[[事代主]]）と[[シタテルヒメ|下照姫命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[建御名方神|建御名方命]]を配祀する。[[葛城氏]]・[[鴨氏]]によって祀られた神社で、[[高鴨神社]]（高鴨社）・[[葛城御歳神社]]（中鴨社）に対して「下鴨社」とも呼ばれる。事代主神は元々は鴨族が信仰していた神であり、当社が事代主神の信仰の本源である。[[大神神社]]（奈良県[[桜井市]]）に祀られる[[大国主]]の子に当たることから、「大神神社の別宮」とも称される。 下照姫命については、事代主とともに祀られることに疑問があることから、元は別の神が祀られていたものとみられる。当社の古い社名は「鴨弥都波（かもみつは）」であり、「鴨の水際（みづは）の神」と解せる。当地は[[葛城川]]と柳田川の合流地点であり、元々は水の神を祀っていたものとする説がある。また、事代主の妹である[[高照姫命]]が祀られていたのが下照姫命と混同されたとする説もある。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[崇神天皇]]の時代、勅命により[[太田田根子]]の孫の大賀茂都美命が創建した。一帯は「鴨都波遺跡」という遺跡で、弥生時代の土器や農具が多数出土しており、古くから鴨族がこの地に住みついて農耕をしていたことがわかる。 [[延喜式神名帳]]では「鴨都波八重事代主命神社 二座」と記載され、名神大社に列している。[[延喜式]]巻三「臨時祭」では「鴨神社 二座」と記されている。 大正10年（[[1927年]]）に県社に列格した。 == 外部リンク == * [http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~kamotuba/ 鴨都波神社]（公式サイト） {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもつはしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名かもつはしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:御所市]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: /* 外部リンク */ 賀茂氏カテゴリ追加、加茂神社カテゴリ追加、加茂神社カテゴリ追加。

下照姫命| 社格=式内社（名神大）・県社| 創建=崇神天皇の時代| 本殿=加茂[[流造]]| 例祭=[[10月9日]]}} '''鴨都波神社'''（かもつばじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[御所市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == 積羽八重事代主命（[[事代主]]）と[[シタテルヒメ|下照姫命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[建御名方神|建御名方命]]を配祀する。[[葛城氏]]・[[鴨氏]]によって祀られた神社で、[[高鴨神社]]（高鴨社）・[[葛城御歳神社]]（中鴨社）に対して「下鴨社」とも呼ばれる。事代主神は元々は鴨族が信仰していた神であり、当社が事代主神の信仰の本源である。[[大神神社]]（奈良県[[桜井市]]）に祀られる[[大国主]]の子に当たることから、「大神神社の別宮」とも称される。 下照姫命については、事代主とともに祀られることに疑問があることから、元は別の神が祀られていたものとみられる。当社の古い社名は「鴨弥都波（かもみつは）」であり、「鴨の水際（みづは）の神」と解せる。当地は[[葛城川]]と柳田川の合流地点であり、元々は水の神を祀っていたものとする説がある。また、事代主の妹である[[高照姫命]]が祀られていたのが下照姫命と混同されたとする説もある。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[崇神天皇]]の時代、勅命により[[太田田根子]]の孫の大賀茂都美命が創建した。一帯は「鴨都波遺跡」という遺跡で、弥生時代の土器や農具が多数出土しており、古くから鴨族がこの地に住みついて農耕をしていたことがわかる。 [[延喜式神名帳]]では「鴨都波八重事代主命神社 二座」と記載され、名神大社に列している。[[延喜式]]巻三「臨時祭」では「鴨神社 二座」と記されている。 大正10年（[[1927年]]）に県社に列格した。 == 文化財 == 鴨都波神社本殿（御所市指定文化財）－棟札によれば天保12年（1841）の建立とされる。 祭礼渡御図絵馬（奈良県指定[[有形民俗文化財]]） 御所の献灯行事（奈良県指定[[無形民俗文化財]]） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもつはしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名かもつはしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:御所市]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:賀茂氏|社かもつはしんしや]] [[Category:葛城氏|社かもつはしんしや]] [[Category:加茂神社]]

revision.comment: /* 祭神 */ 要出典タグを貼り付け。[[延喜式神名帳]]には当社は「鴨都波八重事代主命神社」とあるので、「鴨都波」の「都波」は主[[祭神]]の名「[[積羽八重事代主命]]」の「積羽」に由来する可能性があるため。
revision.text:

社殿 |所在地=[[奈良県]][[御所市]]宮前町514 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 37.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 58.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神=[[事代主|積羽八重事代主命]]
[[シタテルヒメ|下照姫命]] |社格=[[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）
旧[[県社]] |創建=[[崇神天皇]]の時代 |本殿=加茂[[流造]] |例祭=[[10月9日]] |地図= Japan Nara }} '''鴨都波神社'''（かもつばじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[御所市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == 積羽八重事代主命（[[事代主]]）と[[シタテルヒメ|下照姫命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[建御名方神|建御名方命]]を配祀する。[[葛城氏]]・[[鴨氏]]によって祀られた神社で、[[高鴨神社]]（高鴨社）・[[葛城御歳神社]]（中鴨社）に対して「下鴨社」とも呼ばれる。事代主神は元々は鴨族が信仰していた神であり、当社が事代主神の信仰の本源である。[[大神神社]]（奈良県[[桜井市]]）に祀られる[[大国主]]の子に当たることから、「大神神社の別宮」とも称される。 下照姫命については、事代主とともに祀られることに疑問があることから、元は別の神が祀られていたものとみられる。{{要出典|範囲=当社の古い社名は「鴨弥都波（かもみつは）」であり、「鴨の水際（みづは）の神」と解せる。|date=2018年5月}}当地は[[葛城川]]と柳田川の合流地点であり、{{要出典|範囲=元々は水の神を祀っていたものとする説がある|date=2018年5月}}。{{要出典|範囲=また、事代主の妹である[[高照姫命]]が祀られていたのが下照姫命と混同されたとする説もある。|date=2018年5月}} == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[崇神天皇]]の時代、勅命により[[太田田根子]]の孫の大賀茂都美命が創建した。一帯は「鴨都波遺跡」という遺跡で、弥生時代の土器や農具が多数出土しており、古くから鴨族がこの地に住みついて農耕をしていたことがわかる。 [[延喜式神名帳]]では「鴨都波八重事代主命神社 二座」と記載され、名神大社に列している。[[延喜式]]巻三「臨時祭」では「鴨神社 二座」と記されている。 大正10年（[[1927年]]）に県社に列格した。 == 文化財 == *鴨都波神社本殿（御所市指定文化財）－棟札によれば天保12年（1841）の建立とされる。 *祭礼渡御図絵馬（奈良県指定[[有形民俗文化財]]） *御所の献灯行事（奈良県指定[[無形民俗文化財]]） == 境内 == ファイル:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|鳥居 ファイル:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|本殿（御所市指定文化財） == 外部リンク == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotuba.com//　鴨都波神社]（公式ホームページ） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもつはしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名かもつはしんしや]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:賀茂氏|社かもつはしんしや]] [[Category:葛城氏|社かもつはしんしや]] [[Category:加茂神社]]

旧[[県社]] |創建=[[崇神天皇]]の時代 |本殿=加茂[[流造]] |別名=下鴨社 |例祭=[[10月9日]] |地図= Japan Nara }} '''鴨都波神社'''（かもつばじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[御所市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。[[高鴨神社]]（上鴨社）・[[葛木御歳神社]]（中鴨社）に対して「下鴨社」と称される。 == 祭神 == [[積羽八重事代主命]]と[[下照姫命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[建御名方命]]を配祀する。[[葛城氏]]・[[鴨氏]]によって祀られた神社で、[[高鴨神社]]（高鴨社）・[[葛城御歳神社]]（中鴨社）に対して「下鴨社」とも呼ばれる。事代主神は元々は[[鴨族]]が信仰していた神であり、当社が事代主神の信仰の本源である。[[大神神社]]（奈良県[[桜井市]]）に祀られる[[大国主]]の子に当たることから、「大神神社の別宮」とも称される。 == 立地 == 当地は[[葛城川]]と柳田川の合流地点である。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[崇神天皇]]の時代、勅命により[[太田田根子]]の孫の大賀茂都美命が創建した。一帯は「鴨都波遺跡」という遺跡で、弥生時代の土器や農具が多数出土しており、古くから鴨族がこの地に住みついて農耕をしていたことがわかる。 [[延喜式神名帳]]では「鴨都波八重事代主命神社 二座」と記載され、名神大社に列している。[[延喜式]]巻三「臨時祭」では「鴨神社 二座」と記されている。 大正10年（[[1927年]]）に県社に列格した。 == 文化財 == *鴨都波神社本殿（御所市指定文化財）－棟札によれば天保12年（1841）の建立とされる。 *祭礼渡御図絵馬（奈良県指定[[有形民俗文化財]]） *御所の献灯行事（奈良県指定[[無形民俗文化財]]） == 境内 == ファイル:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|鳥居 ファイル:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|本殿（御所市指定文化財） == 外部リンク == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotuba.com//　鴨都波神社]（公式ホームページ） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもつはしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名かもつはしんしや]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:賀茂氏|社かもつはしんしや]] [[Category:葛城氏|社かもつはしんしや]] [[Category:加茂神社]]

旧[[県社]] |創建=[[崇神天皇]]の時代 |本殿=加茂[[流造]] |別名=下鴨社 |例祭=[[10月9日]] |地図= Japan Nara }} [[画像:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''鴨都波神社'''（かもつばじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[御所市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。[[高鴨神社]]（上鴨社）・[[葛木御歳神社]]（中鴨社）に対して「下鴨社」と称される。 == 祭神 == [[積羽八重事代主命]]と[[下照姫命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[建御名方命]]を配祀する。[[葛城氏]]・[[鴨氏]]によって祀られた神社で、[[高鴨神社]]（高鴨社）・[[葛城御歳神社]]（中鴨社）に対して「下鴨社」とも呼ばれる。事代主神は元々は[[鴨族]]が信仰していた神であり、当社が事代主神の信仰の本源である。[[大神神社]]（奈良県[[桜井市]]）に祀られる[[大物主]]の子に当たることから、「大神神社の別宮」とも称される。 == 立地 == 当地は[[葛城川]]と柳田川の合流地点である。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[崇神天皇]]の時代、勅命により[[大田田根子命]]の孫の[[大賀茂都美命]]が創建した。一帯は「鴨都波遺跡」という遺跡で、弥生時代の土器や農具が多数出土しており、古くから鴨族がこの地に住みついて農耕をしていたことがわかる。 [[延喜式神名帳]]では「鴨都波八重事代主命神社 二座」と記載され、名神大社に列している。[[延喜式]]巻三「臨時祭」では「鴨神社 二座」と記されている。 大正10年（[[1927年]]）に県社に列格した。 == 文化財 == *鴨都波神社本殿（御所市指定文化財）－棟札によれば天保12年（1841）の建立とされる。 *祭礼渡御図絵馬（奈良県指定[[有形民俗文化財]]） *御所の献灯行事（奈良県指定[[無形民俗文化財]]） == 境内 == ファイル:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|本殿（御所市指定文化財） == 外部リンク == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotuba.com　鴨都波神社] - 公式ホームページ * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html　鴨都波神社] - 御所市観光ガイド * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002　鴨都波八重事代主命神社二座] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもつはしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名かもつはしんしや]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:賀茂氏|社かもつはしんしや]] [[Category:葛城氏|社かもつはしんしや]] [[Category:加茂神社]]

{{Shrine |Name=Kamotsuba Shrine |Image=[[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Shaden.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location=[[Nara Prefecture]][[Gose City]] Miyamae-cho 514 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 27 | Latitude seconds = 37.9 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 58.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | Deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} | Shrine rank={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | Founded=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | Annual Festival={{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Omiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == Location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamo Tsumi no Mikoto|ja|大賀茂都美命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[category:Unsorted link categories]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Kk]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

{{Shrine |Name=Kamotsuba Shrine |Image=[[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Shaden.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location=[[Nara Prefecture]][[Gose City]] Miyamae-cho 514 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 27 | Latitude seconds = 37.9 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 58.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | Deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} | Shrine rank={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | Founded=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | Annual Festival={{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Omiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == Location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[category:Unsorted link categories]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Kk]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |location=[[Nara Prefecture]][[Gose City]] Miyamae-cho 514 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 27 | Latitude seconds = 37.9 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 58.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | Deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} | Shrine rank={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | consecrated=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival={{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Omiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:images]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

{{Shrine |name=Kamotsuba Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Shaden.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |location=[[Nara Prefecture]][[Gose City]] Miyamae-cho 514 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 27 | Latitude seconds = 37.9 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 58.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | Deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} | Shrine rank={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival={{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Omiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:images]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

{{Shrine |name=Kamotsuba Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Shaden.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |location=[[Nara Prefecture]][[Gose City]] Miyamae-cho 514 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 27 | Latitude seconds = 37.9 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 58.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | Deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} | Shrine rank={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival={{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Omiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Omiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

{{Shrine |name=Kamotsuba Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Shaden.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |location=[[Nara Prefecture]][[Gose City]] Miyamae-cho 514 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 27 | Latitude seconds = 37.9 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 58.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | Deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} | Shrine rank={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival={{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Omiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kamo Tsuba Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[ja:鴨都波神社]]

{{Shrine |name=Kamotsuba Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Shaden.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |location=[[Nara Prefecture]][[Gose City]] Miyamae-cho 514 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 27 | Latitude seconds = 37.9 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 58.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} | Shrine rank={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival={{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Omiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamo Tsuba Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[ja:鴨都波神社]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Omiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Omiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. {{Shrine |name=Kamotsuba Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Shaden.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |location=[[Nara Prefecture]][[Gose City]] Miyamae-cho 514 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 27 | Latitude seconds = 37.9 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 58.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} | Shrine rank={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival={{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Omiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |location=[[Nara Prefecture]][[Gose City]] Miyamae-cho 514 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 27 | Latitude seconds = 37.9 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 58.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} | Shrine rank={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival={{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Omiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Omiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}. In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - {{Shrine |name=Kamotsuba Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Shaden.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |location=[[Nara Prefecture]][[Gose City]] Miyamae-cho 514 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 27 | Latitude seconds = 37.9 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 58.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} | Shrine rank={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival={{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Omiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} | Shrine rank={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival={{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Omiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子命}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - revision.comment: Text replacement - "|大田田根子" to "|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko"
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name=Kamotsuba Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Shaden.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |location=[[Nara Prefecture]][[Gose City]] Miyamae-cho 514 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 27 | Latitude seconds = 37.9 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 58.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} | Shrine rank={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival={{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Omiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]][[Category:New]]
