revision.text:
{{Shrine |name=Kamotsuba Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Shaden.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |location=[[Nara Prefecture]][[Gose City]] Miyamae-cho 514 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 27 | Latitude seconds = 37.9 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 58.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} | Shrine rank={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival={{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Omiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} | Shrine rank={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival={{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日|12=draft|12=draft}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft|12=draft}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主|12=draft|12=draft}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Omiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|12=draft|12=draft}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|12=draft|12=draft}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=draft|12=draft}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主|12=draft|12=draft}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Omiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|12=draft|12=draft}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|12=draft|12=draft}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft|12=draft}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主|12=draft|12=draft}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Omiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|12=draft|12=draft}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|12=draft|12=draft}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=draft|12=draft}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

{{Shrine |name=Kamotsuba Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Shaden.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |location=[[Nara Prefecture]][[Gose City]] Miyamae-cho 514 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 27 | Latitude seconds = 37.9 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 58.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} | Shrine rank={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival={{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日|12=draft|12=draft}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft|12=draft}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主|12=draft|12=draft}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|12=draft}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|12=draft|12=draft}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|12=draft|12=draft}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=draft|12=draft}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主|12=draft|12=draft}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|12=draft}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|12=draft|12=draft}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|12=draft|12=draft}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=draft|12=draft}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

{{Shrine |name=Kamotsuba Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Shaden.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |location=[[Nara Prefecture]][[Gose City]] Miyamae-cho 514 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 27 | Latitude seconds = 37.9 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 58.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival={{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日|12=draft|12=draft}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft|12=draft}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主|12=draft|12=draft}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|12=draft|12=draft}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|12=draft|12=draft}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=draft|12=draft}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name=Kamotsuba Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Shaden.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |location=[[Nara Prefecture]][[Gose City]] Miyamae-cho 514 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 27 | Latitude seconds = 37.9 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 58.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival={{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival={{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 9|qq=}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai-sha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|qq=}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|qq=}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuninushi|qq=}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa Shrine|qq=}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi River|qq=}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|qq=}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|qq=}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

{{Shrine |name=Kamotsuba Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Shaden.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |location=[[Nara Prefecture]][[Gose City]] Miyamae-cho 514 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 27 | Latitude seconds = 37.9 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 58.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival={{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 9|qq=}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|qq=}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|qq=}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuninushi|qq=}}, who is worshipped at {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa Shrine|qq=}} (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi River|qq=}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}} under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|qq=}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|qq=}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

It was worshipped by the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]] and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Okuninushi]], who is worshipped at [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi River|qq=}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|qq=}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|qq=}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|qq=}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|qq=}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]] and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Okuninushi]], who is worshipped at [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi River|qq=}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|qq=}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|qq=}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|qq=}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|qq=}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]] and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Okuninushi]], who is worshipped at [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi River|qq=}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|qq=}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|qq=}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]]

{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}} 2 seats)

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|qq=}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|qq=}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]] and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Okuninushi]], who is worshipped at [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (in [[Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi River|qq=}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|qq=}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|qq=}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myojin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Gose City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose City]] [[Category:Kamo clan shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan shrines]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamo Tsuba Shrine]] [[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:賀茂朝臣氏]]
