{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シタテルヒメ}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival=[[:en:October 9|October 9]] | Map=Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Gose|WD=Q847334}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.460528|N|135.732861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|1=Takakamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673295}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|qq=}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シタテルヒメ}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]] and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Okuninushi]], who is worshipped at [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (in [[:en:Sakurai|Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi River|1=Katsuragi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620895}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大鴨積命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myōjin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:History of Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] {{Commons category|Kamotsuba-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11675614}}

Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival=[[:en:October 9|October 9]] | Map=Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Gose|WD=Q847334}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.460528|N|135.732861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|1=Takakamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673295}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|qq=}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シタテルヒメ}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]] and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Okuninushi]], who is worshipped at [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (in [[:en:Sakurai|Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi River|1=Katsuragi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620895}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大鴨積命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myōjin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:History of Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] {{Commons category|Kamotsuba-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11675614}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:History of Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] {{Commons category|Kamotsuba-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11675614}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Kamotsuba-jinja]][[ja:鴨都波神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11675614]] Wikidata name: Kamotsuba-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11675614]] Wikidata name: Kamotsuba-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Kamotsuba Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Kamotsuba-jinja haiden.jpg |location=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[Gose City]] Miyamae-cho 514 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 27 | Latitude seconds = 37.9 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 58.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}
{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シタテルヒメ}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival=[[:en:October 9|October 9]] | Map=Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Gose|WD=Q847334}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.460528|N|135.732861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|1=Takakamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673295}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|qq=}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シタテルヒメ}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]] and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Okuninushi]], who is worshipped at [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (in [[:en:Sakurai|Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi River|1=Katsuragi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620895}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大鴨積命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myōjin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:History of Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] {{Commons category|Kamotsuba-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11675614}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|1=Takakamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673295}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|qq=}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シタテルヒメ}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]] and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Okuninushi]], who is worshipped at [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (in [[:en:Sakurai|Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi River|1=Katsuragi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620895}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大鴨積命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myōjin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:History of Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] {{Commons category|Kamotsuba-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11675614}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myōjin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:History of Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] {{Commons category|Kamotsuba-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11675614}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シタテルヒメ}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival=[[:en:October 9|October 9]] | Map=Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Gose|WD=Q847334}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.460528|N|135.732861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|1=Takakamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673295}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|qq=}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シタテルヒメ}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]] and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Okuninushi]], who is worshipped at [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (in [[:en:Sakurai|Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi River|1=Katsuragi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620895}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大鴨積命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myōjin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シタテルヒメ}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival=[[:en:October 9|October 9]] | Map=Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Gose|WD=Q847334}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.460528|N|135.732861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|1=Takakamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673295}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|qq=}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シタテルヒメ}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]] and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Okuninushi]], who is worshipped at [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (in [[:en:Sakurai|Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi River|1=Katsuragi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620895}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大鴨積命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myōjin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:History of Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] {{Commons category|Kamotsuba-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11675614}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival=[[:en:October 9|October 9]] | Map=Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Gose|WD=Q847334}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.460528|N|135.732861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|1=Takakamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673295}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|qq=}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シタテルヒメ}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]] and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Okuninushi]], who is worshipped at [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (in [[:en:Sakurai|Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi River|1=Katsuragi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620895}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大鴨積命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myōjin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:History of Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] {{Commons category|Kamotsuba-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11675614}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:History of Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] {{Commons category|Kamotsuba-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11675614}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[commons:Category:Kamotsuba-jinja]][[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[commons:Category:Kamotsuba-jinja]][[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[commons:Category:Kamotsuba-jinja]][[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kamotsuba-jinja]][[ja:鴨都波神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11675614]] Wikidata name: Kamotsuba-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11675614]] Wikidata name: Kamotsuba-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Kamotsuba Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Kamotsuba-jinja haiden.jpg |location=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[Gose City]] Miyamae-cho 514 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 27 | Latitude seconds = 37.9 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 58.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}
{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シタテルヒメ}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival=[[:en:October 9|October 9]] | Map=Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Gose|WD=Q847334}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.460528|N|135.732861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|1=Takakamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673295}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|qq=}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シタテルヒメ}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]] and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Okuninushi]], who is worshipped at [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (in [[:en:Sakurai|Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi River|1=Katsuragi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620895}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大鴨積命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myōjin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:History of Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] {{Commons category|Kamotsuba-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11675614}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シタテルヒメ}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival=[[:en:October 9|October 9]] | Map=Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Gose|WD=Q847334}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.460528|N|135.732861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|1=Takakamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673295}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|qq=}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シタテルヒメ}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]] and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Okuninushi]], who is worshipped at [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (in [[:en:Sakurai|Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi River|1=Katsuragi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620895}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大鴨積命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myōjin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:鴨都波神社]] {{Commons category|Kamotsuba-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11675614}} [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myōjin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鴨都波神社]] {{Commons category|Kamotsuba-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11675614}} [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kamotsuba-jinja]][[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamotsuba-jinja]][[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamotsuba-jinja]][[ja:鴨都波神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kamotsuba-jinja]][[ja:鴨都波神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11675614]] Wikidata name: Kamotsuba-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11675614]] Wikidata name: Kamotsuba-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Kamotsuba Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Kamotsuba-jinja haiden.jpg |location=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[Gose City]] Miyamae-cho 514 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 27 | Latitude seconds = 37.9 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 43 | Longitude seconds = 58.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}
{{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シタテルヒメ}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} 2 seats)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year=Era of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Kamo-style [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alias=Shimogamo Shrine | festival=[[:en:October 9|October 9]] | Map=Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Gose|WD=Q847334}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.460528|N|135.732861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Kamotsuba Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamotsuba Shrine''' (鴨都波神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[Gose City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also referred to as "Shimogamo Shrine," in contrast to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|1=Takakamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673295}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and {{ill|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|ja|葛木御歳神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine|qq=}} (Nakagamo Shrine). == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|積羽八重事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シタテルヒメ}} as its main deities, with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} enshrined as a subordinate deity. It was worshipped by the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]] and the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. Kotoshironushi, originally revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan, has its primary place of worship at this shrine. Since Kotoshironushi is considered the child of [[:en:Ōmononushi|Okuninushi]], who is worshipped at [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (in [[:en:Sakurai|Sakurai]], Nara), this shrine is sometimes referred to as a "branch shrine" of Omiwa Shrine. == location == The shrine is situated at the confluence of the {{ill|Katsuragi River|ja|葛城川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi River|1=Katsuragi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620895}} and the Yanagida River. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] under the orders of the emperor, and the founder was {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大賀茂都美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大鴨積命}}, grandson of {{ill|Otataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}}. The surrounding area is an archaeological site known as "Kamotsuba Ruins," where numerous Yayoi-period pottery and farming tools have been excavated, providing evidence of early agricultural activities by the Kamo clan in this region. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats," and is classified as a Myōjin Taisha. It is also mentioned in Volume 3 of the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], "Rinji-sai," as "Kamo Shrine, 2 seats." In 1927 (Taisho 16), it was officially designated as a Ken-sha. == Cultural Properties == * Main Hall of Kamotsuba Shrine (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) – According to the ridge tag, it was built in 1841 (Tenpo 12). * Festival Procession Painting Votive Plaque (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) * Gose Lantern Festival (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Nara Prefecture) == Grounds == File:Kamotsuba Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall File:Kamotsuba_Jinja_Honden.jpg|Main Hall (Designated Cultural Property of Gose City) == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kamotsuba-jinja}} * [http://kamotsuba.com Kamotsuba Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001391.html Kamotsuba Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26002 Kamotsuba Yahiro Kotoshironushi no Mikoto Shrine, 2 seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources" {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鴨都波神社|version=101062575|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鴨都波神社]] {{Commons category|Kamotsuba-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11675614}} [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
